raw_response:
I **agree** with the chosen answer (C). My additional point reinforces why macula adherens would not typically coexist alongside zona occludens within the same region due to their specialized functions—macula adherens primarily provides stability and organization among cells, whereas zona occludens regulates paracellular transport. Given these differing roles, they can exist independently depending on cellular needs, but generally do so in separate regions of epithelial tissues. Therefore, my agreement solidifies option C as correct because the absence of macula adherens fits well with its distinct role compared to other listed junctional types.

Answer Choice: C.
